auto_approve.mechanism: no auto-approve / always-allow field documented in Configuration Schema

enterprise_management.mechanism: only user-level and project-level configuration scopes documented; no organization/enterprise admin layer

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Configuration schema shows env field for stdio servers but no expansion syntax (e.g. ${VAR}) is documented

marketplace.mechanism: Quick Start: Add from Registry — 40+ pre-configured servers organized into categories (Development & Testing, Project Management, Payments, Design, Infrastructure)

oauth_support.mechanism: OAuth tokens documented under 'OAuth Tokens' heading; most HTTP servers support OAuth with token storage in system keyring

resource_referencing.mechanism: no @-mention or resource referencing syntax for MCP resources documented

tool_filtering.mechanism: per-server tool disable via disabledTools field in Configuration Schema

transport_types.mechanism: two transports documented: http (remote endpoints) and stdio (local processes); 'Adding HTTP Servers' and 'Adding Stdio Servers' headings

providerExtensions.name: Interactive MCP Manager (/mcp)
providerExtensions.summary: Interactive Manager invoked via /mcp slash command for connecting and managing MCP servers without manual JSON editing.

providerExtensions.name: Factory as MCP Server
providerExtensions.summary: The Factory CLI can itself run as an MCP server, exposing its capabilities to other tools that speak the MCP client protocol.

providerExtensions.name: Categorized server registry
providerExtensions.summary: Pre-configured servers organized into categories (Development & Testing, Project Management & Documentation, Payments & Commerce, Design & Media, Infrastructure & DevOps) for discovery via Quick Start: Add from Registry.
